perturbation.original_text: "Conf idential Information " means any information disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by inspection of tangible objects, including without limitation documents, prototypes, samples, plant and equipme nt, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, software, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration, marketing materials or finances, which is designated as "Confidential," "Proprietary" or some similar designation.
perturbation.changed_text: "Conf idential Information " means any data disclosed by either party to the other party, either directly or indirectly, in writing, orally or by inspection of tangible objects, including without limitation documents, prototypes, samples, plant and equipme nt, research, product plans, products, services, customer lists, software, developments, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration, marketing materials or finances, which is designated as "Secret," "Restricted" or some similar designation.
perturbation.explanation: The terms "Confidential" and "Proprietary" are replaced by "Secret" and "Restricted" while also changing the term "Information" with "Data" which introduces a contradiction, as later parts of the agreement refer specifically to 'Confidential Information' and not 'Secret Data', leading to ambiguity about what is protected under the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction on what the meaning of 'Confidential Information' truly means.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall not use the Confidential Information of the other party for any purpose except to evaluate and engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship between the parties. Neither party shall disclose any Confidential Information of the other party to third parties. If any party makes copies of the Confidential Information of the other party, such copies shall also constitute Confidential Information and any and all confid ential markings on such documents shall be maintained.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Confidential Information' by replacing 'Confidential' and 'Proprietary' with 'Secret' and 'Restricted' and replaces 'Information' with 'Data'. The contradicted text refers specifically to 'Confidential Information'. Since the definition of 'Confidential Information' has been altered, it creates a contradiction because the contradicted text relies on the original definition, making it unclear if the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' still apply, as the definition has been modified to 'Secret Data'. This leads to ambiguity and potential unenforceability, consistent with the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential information, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Confidential Information of which it becomes aware.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will employ suitable methods to safeguard and prevent the disclosure and unauthorized use of the Proprietary Data of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party will take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential data, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Privileged Material of which it becomes aware.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'Confidential Information' is replaced by 'Proprietary Data' and then 'Privileged Material'. This creates ambiguity about whether these terms are intended to be synonymous. The original text focused on protecting 'Confidential Information,' but the modification introduces 'Proprietary Data' and 'Privileged Material', which are undefined and may not cover the same scope of information. This is contradicting the obligations defined in the paragraph as well as in the original definition paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party will employ suitable methods to safeguard and prevent the disclosure and unauthorized use of the Proprietary Data of the other party. Without limiting the foregoing, each party will take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own most highly confidential data, and shall promptly notify the disclosing party of any misuse or misappropriation of Privileged Material of which it becomes aware. Each party sh all disclose Confidential Information only to those officers, directors, employees and contractors who are required to have the information in order to evaluate or engage in discussions concerning the contemplated business relationship, and such party shal l remain responsible for compliance with the terms of this Agreement by its officers, directors, employees and contractors.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' while the changed text introduces 'Proprietary Data' and 'Privileged Material.' The contradicted text then uses all three terms ('Proprietary Data', 'Privileged Material', and 'Confidential Information'). The perturbation explanation indicates that these terms are undefined and may not have the same scope, leading to ambiguity and conflicting obligations. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, creates a strong in-text contradiction, making it unclear which information is subject to which protection or disclosure obligations. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize the sections as incompatible due to the mix of three terms that should only be one.

perturbation.original_text: All documents and other tangible objects containing or representing Confidential Information which have been disclosed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and re main the property of the disclosing party and shall be promptly returned to the disclosing party upon the disclosing party's written request.
perturbation.changed_text: All records and other tangible objects holding or representing Sensitive Data which have been revealed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and re main the possession of the originating party and shall be promptly returned to the originating party upon the originating party's formal demand.
perturbation.explanation: The alteration substitutes 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Data', 'disclosing party' with 'originating party', and 'written request' with 'formal demand'. Because 'Sensitive Data' and 'originating party' are not defined, this substitution confuses the obligations concerning the return of materials, thereby contradicting the original meaning. The original term of "Confidential Information" isn't used anymore, it is being contradited due to its replacement with "Sensitive Data"
perturbation.contradicted_text: All records and other tangible objects holding or representing Sensitive Data which have been revealed by either party to the other party, and all copies thereof which are in the possession of the other party, shall be and re main the possession of the originating party and shall be promptly returned to the originating party upon the originating party's formal demand.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces defined terms ('Confidential Information', 'disclosing party', 'written request') with undefined terms ('Sensitive Data', 'originating party', 'formal demand'). Since 'Sensitive Data' and 'originating party' are not explicitly defined within the contract, the altered section introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which uses these undefined terms. This creates a contradiction because the obligations surrounding the return of materials are now unclear, rendering the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The change directly breaks compatibility, as the original definitions no longer apply and the new terms lack clarity.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of each receiving party hereunder shall surviv e for a period of five years after the disclosure of the Confidential Information or until such time as all Confidential Information of the other party disclosed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving party, whichever is earlier.
perturbation.changed_text: The duties of each receiving entity hereunder shall continu e for a period of five years after the revelation of the Protected Data or until such time as all Protected Data of the other party revealed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving entity, whichever comes first.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'obligations' to 'duties', 'receiving party' to 'receiving entity', 'disclosure of Confidential Information' to 'revelation of Protected Data', and 'whichever is earlier' to 'whichever comes first' creates confusion. 'Protected Data' is not defined, contradicting the intended scope and duration of confidentiality. It is creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The duties of each receiving entity hereunder shall continu e for a period of five years after the revelation of the Protected Data or until such time as all Protected Data of the other party revealed hereunder becomes publicly known and made generally available through no action or inaction of the receiving entity, whichever comes first. Parties agree to honor all patent protections.
perturbation.justification: The explanation states that the change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Data' creates confusion because 'Protected Data' is not defined. The contradicted text uses the term 'Protected Data'. This lack of definition creates an in-text contradiction as per the definition of Misaligned Terminology where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The original text uses 'Confidential Information' which implies that it's properly defined elsewhere in the contract (or understood), whereas the changed text replaces it with an undefined term, thus causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This document contains the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and neither party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other party except as set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This record contains the complete accord between the participants regarding the concern discussed herein, and no participant shall have any duty, stated or suggested by law, concerning trade secret or exclusive intelligence of the other participant except as specified herein.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'document' with 'record,' 'agreement' with 'accord,' 'parties' with 'participants,' 'subject matter' with 'concern discussed,' 'obligation' with 'duty,' 'express or implied' with 'stated or suggested,' and 'proprietary information' with 'exclusive intelligence' introduces substantial terminological inconsistency. The use of 'record,' 'accord,' 'participants,' and 'exclusive intelligence' are undefined and create confusion with the original terms. Because "participants" is being used, it is not clear that this also means the Parties of the contract, therefore, contradiction the enforcement and meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This record contains the complete accord between the participants regarding the concern discussed herein, and no participant shall have any duty, stated or suggested by law, concerning trade secret or exclusive intelligence of the other participant except as specified herein. The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement, or any of its terms or provisions, will not affect the validity of this Agreement as a whole, which will at all times remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces key legal terms like 'agreement' and 'parties' with undefined terms like 'accord' and 'participants.' This creates ambiguity because it is unclear if 'participants' refers to the 'Parties' defined elsewhere in the contract, thus potentially invalidating the clause that ensures the agreement remains in full force and effect. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition where using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
